questions.questionText: A straight line has point O on it. A ray OC is drawn from O such that $\angle AOC$ is 65°. What is the measure of $\angle BOC$?
questions.explanation: Angles on a straight line sum to 180°. So, $\angle BOC$ = 180° - 65° = 115°.

questions.questionText: Two straight lines AB and CD intersect at point O. If $\angle AOC$ is 40°, what is the measure of $\angle BOD$?
questions.explanation: Vertically opposite angles are equal. Since $\angle AOC$ and $\angle BOD$ are vertically opposite, $\angle BOD$ = 40°.

questions.questionText: Three angles around a point are x, 2x, and 3x. What is the value of x?
questions.explanation: Angles around a point sum to 360°. So, x + $2x + 3x = 360$°, which means $6x = 360$°. Therefore, $x = 60$°.

questions.questionText: In the diagram, lines AB and CD are parallel. Line EF is a transversal intersecting AB at G and CD at H. If $\angle AGE$ is 70°, what is the measure of $\angle GHC$?
questions.explanation: $\angle AGE$ and $\angle GHC$ are corresponding angles. Corresponding angles are equal when lines are parallel. So, $\angle GHC$ = 70°.

questions.questionText: In the diagram, lines PQ and RS are parallel. Line TU is a transversal intersecting PQ at X and RS at Y. If $\angle PXY$ is 120°, what is the measure of $\angle XYR$?
questions.explanation: $\angle PXY$ and $\angle QXY$ form a straight line, so $\angle QXY$ = 180° - 120° = 60°. $\angle QXY$ and $\angle XYR$ are alternate interior angles, which are equal when lines are parallel. Therefore, $\angle XYR$ = 60°.

questions.questionText: Lines MN and OP are parallel. Line QR is a transversal intersecting MN at S and OP at T. If $\angle MST$ is 110°, what is the measure of $\angle STP$?
questions.explanation: $\angle MST$ and $\angle STP$ are interior angles on the same side of the transversal. Their sum is 180° when lines are parallel. So, $\angle STP$ = 180° - 110° = 70°.

questions.questionText: A triangle has angles measuring 50° and 75°. What is the measure of the third angle?
questions.explanation: The sum of angles in a triangle is 180°. So, the third angle = 180° - (50° + 75°) = 180° - 125° = 55°.

questions.questionText: An isosceles triangle has its apex angle measuring 80°. What is the measure of one of its base angles?
questions.explanation: In an isosceles triangle, the two base angles are equal. If the apex angle is 80°, then the sum of the two base angles is 180° - 80° = 100°. Therefore, each base angle is 100° / 2 = 50°.

questions.questionText: In triangle ABC, side BC is extended to point D. If $\angle ABC$ = 60° and $\angle BAC$ = 70°, what is the measure of the exterior $\angle ACD$?
questions.explanation: The exterior angle of a triangle is equal to the sum of the two opposite interior angles. So, $\angle ACD$ = $\angle ABC$ + $\angle BAC$ = 60° + 70° = 130°.

questions.questionText: A quadrilateral has interior angles x, 2x, 3x, and 4x. What is the value of x?
questions.explanation: The sum of interior angles in a quadrilateral is 360°. So, x + $2x + 3x + 4x = 360$°, which means $10x = 360$°. Therefore, $x = 36$°.

questions.questionText: In a diagram, lines AB and CD are parallel. A transversal line EF intersects AB at X and CD at Y. Point Z is on line CD such that triangle XYZ is formed (with vertices X, Y, Z). If $\angle EXB$ = 70° and $\angle XZY$ = 60°, find the measure of $\angle YXZ$.
questions.explanation: $\angle EXB$ = 70°. $\angle AXY$ is vertically opposite to $\angle EXB$, so $\angle AXY$ = 70°. Since AB is parallel to CD, $\angle XYD$ and $\angle AXY$ are alternate interior angles, so $\angle XYD$ = 70°. In triangle XYZ, YZ is a segment of line CD, so $\angle XYZ$ (the angle at Y in the triangle) is 70°. We are given $\angle XZY$ = 60°. The sum of angles in a triangle is 180°. Therefore, $\angle YXZ$ = 180° - ($\angle XYZ$ + $\angle XZY$) = 180° - (70° + 60°) = 180° - 130° = 50°.

questions.questionText: In triangle ABC, angle A = (2x + 10)°, angle B = (3x - 5)°, and angle C = (x + 25)°. Find the value of x.
questions.explanation: The sum of angles in a triangle is 180°. So, (2x + 10) + (3x - 5) + (x + 25) = 180. Combining like terms: $6x + 30 = 180$. Subtract 30 from both sides: $6x = 150$. Divide by 6: $x = 25$.
